race.name: Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo
race.display_name: Tour of Thekkady — Kerala Gran Fondo

climate.description: Tropical warmth in March dry season, ideal for long rides with lush scenery.

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 73. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

course_description.character: A scenic rollercoaster of relentless climbs through Kerala's lush plantations, serene lakes, iconic dams, and wildlife-rich forests, blending Tour de France-style elevation with tropical tranquility on smooth, low-traffic roads.
course_description.signature_challenge: The 3000m+ elevation gain over 145km, replicating TdF stage difficulty amid Kerala's ever-changing landscapes from backwaters to highland forests.
suffering_zones: Kulamavu and Idukki Dams Climb: Steep ascents crossing massive dams after passing through Pala and Muttom, with 1000m+ cumulative gain in exposed, panoramic sections.
Cardamom Estates to Kattappana: Long, grinding pulls through aromatic spice hills and rubber plantations, heat-building mid-route where fatigue sets in amid stunning vistas.
Final Push to Thekkady: Rainforest finale with punchy rollers into wildlife sanctuary, testing legs after 3000m total gain.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Kottayam town near Kumarakom backwaters offers hotels/homestays; Thekkady finish has rainforest resorts; book early for event weekend.
logistics.transport: Self-supported Gran Fondo with hydration, medical aid, bag drop from Kottayam to Thekkady, optional bike return; registration open until Feb 16, 2026; categories close at 8-9-14 hours.

history.origin_story: India's premier Gran Fondo launched by KEG Bikers as not-for-profit cyclosportive, aspiring to be largest international cycling event; supported by Kerala Tourism.

final_verdict.one_liner: India's must-do Gran Fondo: TdF climbs meet Kerala paradise.
